title: Samantha's Superpower
author: Samantha & Dady Fredy
subtitle: A Bedtime Story by Samantha & Dady Fredy
dedication: For Samantha — who already flies, every single day.

lines: Fly, Samantha, brave and true,
There's someone waiting just for you.
closing: Love is the wing that never dies.

pages.title: Samantha's Superpower
pages.subtitle: A Bedtime Story by Samantha & Dady Fredy
pages.dedication: For Samantha — who already flies, every single day.

lines: Samantha lay in bed and sighed,
And whispered to the star outside:
"I wish I had a power, just one —
Wings to fly to everyone."
She closed her eyes. She held her breath.
The room went still. The world went soft.
And somewhere between wake and dream,
Her shoulders glowed with a silver gleam.
Two wings unfolded — feather-light,
As bright as stars, as soft as night.
She flexed them once. She flexed them twice.
They hummed like bells. They smelled like rice.
She picked her phone up from the sheet,
Its little screen a heartbeat's beat.
She typed the words she knew by heart:
"Find Dady Fredy. Show me the start."
A blue dot blinked — then held its place.
She memorized his little space.
"I'm coming, Dady. Hold on tight.
Your anak has her wings tonight."
She opened up the window wide,
And stepped into the starlit ride.
Angeles City shrank below —
The rooftops, streetlamps, Sip & Sing's glow.
She flew past clouds that tasted sweet,
Past airplanes humming in their sleep.
Past oceans stitched with silver thread,
Past mountains wearing snow to bed.
And there — she saw him through the wall.
She heard his voice. She heard him call
The way he always calls at night:
"Goodnight, Samantha. Sleep all right."
She landed softly. Touched his hand.
He looked at her. He couldn't understand.
"Anak — you're here? But how? But why?"
She grinned: "I learned how to fly."
"Now hold my hand," Samantha said.
She closed her eyes and bowed her head.
A shimmer wrapped them — gold and bright —
And both of them dissolved in light.
They reappeared on Kalemegdan's wall,
Where Sava meets the Danube's call.
Belgrade was sleeping — calm and deep —
And every cobblestone was warm beneath their feet.
They walked the streets that Dady knew,
Past bakeries and plum trees, too.
He showed her where his mother played,
The park, the school, the lemonade.
"This is where your tata grew,"
He whispered. "Now it belongs to you."
Samantha held his hand and smiled.
Belgrade had waited for this child.
But even superpowers fade with dawn,
And even magic must move on.
She felt the dream begin to thin,
The wings grew soft, the light grew dim.
"Don't worry, Dady," she said, so clear.
"I'll fly to you. I'll always be here.
Whenever you're lonely, whenever you're sad —
Just look at the sky. Your anak's not bad."
He laughed. He cried. He held her tight.
And whispered the words he says every night:
"You don't need wings, anak, don't you see?
You already flew — straight into me."
Samantha opened her eyes in bed.
The phone was dark. The wings had fled.
But something warm was in her hand —
A tiny stone from Belgrade's land.
So close your bright eyes now, my Samantha, my own,
You carried your Dady all the way home.
You don't need a cape or a crown or a key —
The strongest superpower was always just we.
Goodnight, Samantha.
Goodnight, anak.
